cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about a search group formed by American scientists and what they found or did. First, I need to recall the relevant part of the text. The passage mentions that in 1975, some American scientists formed a search group. They used an underwater camera that took pictures every seventy seconds. Some of the pictures seemed to show a red-brown creature with a four-meter body and a four-meter neck. Because of this, many people began to believe in the monster. However, even today we can't be certain. Now, looking at the options: A) found the monster itself — The text says the pictures seemed to show a creature, but it's not confirmed as the monster. The answer isn't A because they didn't find the actual monster, just possible evidence. B) found a huge cave under water — The passage mentions a huge underwater cave found in an earlier effort (before 1961 or in the decade after 1961), but the American scientists in 1975 didn't find the cave. So B is not correct. C) believed that there wasn't any monster at all — The text says the pictures made people believe in
